catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-1-q1.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“industrial processes that create raw materials for concrete”

Paragraph G:

fly ash = byproduct of coal-burning power plants
slag = byproduct of iron-ore smelting

→ giải thích các industrial processes tạo ra materials thay thế cement.

→ Đáp án: G.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-1-q2.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“locations where high-rise wooden buildings can be found”

Paragraph D:
“Vancouver, Vienna and Brumunddal in Norway”

→ examples of cities with tall wooden buildings.

→ Đáp án: D.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-1-q3.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“how widely available the raw materials of concrete are”

Paragraph C:
“ingredients of concrete ... found in abundance in most places around the globe”

→ raw materials widely available.

→ Đáp án: C.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-1-q4.explanation:
Câu hỏi:
“more high-rise wooden buildings are needed...”

Paragraph F:
“only when a wider range of construction projects has been proven in practice”

→ more real examples needed before wood seen as true alternative.

→ Đáp án: F

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-1-q5.explanation:
Câu:
“environmental concerns are encouraging ___ to use wood”

Đoạn:
“climate change is driving architects to turn to treated timber”

→ Đáp án: architects.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-1-q6.explanation:
Câu:
“as ___ in the atmosphere enters wood”

Đoạn:
“Wood expands as it absorbs moisture from the air”

→ Đáp án: moisture.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-1-q7.explanation:
Câu:
“___ of solid wood are glued together”

Đoạn:
“stick layers of solid-sawn timber together”

→ Đáp án: layers.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-1-q8.explanation:
Câu:
“improvement ... in terms of the ___ with which they can be constructed”

Đoạn:
“constructed at a greater speed”

→ Đáp án: speed.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-1-q9.explanation:
C — Anna Surgenor

Statement:
“environmental advantage ... may not be as great”

Surgenor:
transporting materials long distances may cancel out carbon savings.

→ Đáp án: C.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-1-q10.explanation:
A — Chris Cheeseman

Statement:
“hard to create an alternative ... comparable benefits”

Cheeseman:
“Concrete is amazing stuff. Making anything with similar properties is going to be very difficult.”

→ exact match.

→ Đáp án: A.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-1-q11.explanation:
B — Markus Mannstrom

Statement:
“environment worries increased interest in wood”

Mannstrom:
“climate change concerns the key driver”

→ environmental concern drives demand.

→ Đáp án: B.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-1-q12.explanation:
D — Felix Preston and Johanna Lehne

Statement:
“Expense ... negative response to new cements”

Đoạn:
“rarely as cost-effective as conventional cement”

→ expense/cost issue limits acceptance.

→ Đáp án: D.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-1-q13.explanation:
A — Chris Cheeseman

Statement:
“damage caused because produced in huge quantities”

Cheeseman:
“the sheer scale of manufacture is so huge”

→ scale of production is the issue.

→ Đáp án: A.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-2-q14.explanation:
iii — Good and bad aspects of steam technology are passed on

Paragraph A:

steam cars inherited strengths AND weaknesses from railway steam engines.
advantages: quick transport
disadvantages: long heating time, limited water supply

→ heading iii fits best.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-2-q15.explanation:
viii — A better option than the steam car arises

Paragraph B:

Internal Combustion Engine appeared
easier and faster to start
steam cars gradually disappeared

→ new technology replaced steam cars.

→ viii.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-2-q16.explanation:
vi — Positive publicity at last for this quiet, clean, fast vehicle

Paragraph C:

Model B praised in magazines
silent
fast acceleration
cleaner than gasoline cars

→ positive media attention.

→ vi.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-2-q17.explanation:
v — Further improvements lead to commercial orders

Paragraph D:

Model C improved convenience
company received 5,390 deposits/orders

→ improvements resulted in commercial demand.

→ v.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-2-q18.explanation:
vii — A disappointing outcome for customers

Paragraph E:

buyers received faulty cars
sluggish, erratic, reversing wrongly

→ customers disappointed.

→ vii.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-2-q19.explanation:
i — A period in cold conditions before the technology is assessed

Paragraph F:

car left overnight in freezing temperatures
then tested

→ exactly matches heading.

→ i.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-2-q20.explanation:
iv — A possible solution to the issues of today

Paragraph G:

steam cars produce low emissions
writer suggests they may return in future due to environmental concerns

→ possible modern solution.

→ iv.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-2-q21.explanation:
A — Its success was short-lived

Paragraph B:
“But the glory days of steam cars were few.”

→ success existed but did not last long.

→ A.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-2-q22.explanation:
C — needed several attempts to achieve a competitive model

Paragraph C:

first car did not run well
then second and third prototypes built
Model B finally impressive

→ several attempts needed.

→ C.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-2-q23.explanation:
B — raised financial capital

Paragraph D:
Abner sought investment in Detroit and opened company.

→ investment = financial capital.

→ B.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-2-q24.explanation:
Đoạn:
“its maximum speed was pushed to over a hundred miles per hour”

→ later version increased maximum speed.

→ Đáp án: speed.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-2-q25.explanation:
Đoạn:
“fewer than fifty ... had been produced”

→ under fifty cars produced.

→ Đáp án: fifty.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-2-q26.explanation:
Đoạn:
“they are pretty strict”

→ California’s strict emissions laws.

→ Đáp án: strict

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-3-q27.explanation:
B — how a teacher handles a range of learning needs

Đoạn đầu mô tả:

some students need simplified texts
some discuss basic plot
stronger students analyse themes deeply

→ writer dùng ví dụ để cho thấy teacher dealing with mixed abilities in one classroom.

→ Đáp án B.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-3-q28.explanation:
A — It has a very broad appeal

Paragraph 3:
“The practice is intuitively appealing to almost every stakeholder.”

→ almost everyone likes/supports streaming.

→ Đáp án A.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-3-q29.explanation:
C — students not achieving their full potential

Paragraph 5:

brightest students never summit Mount Qomolangma
weaker students don’t enjoy easier walk either

→ both groups fail to reach full potential.

→ Đáp án C.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-3-q30.explanation:
C — the assistance given to a student initially

Paragraph 6:
“provide and then gradually remove this scaffolding”

→ scaffolding = support/help given during learning process.

→ Đáp án C.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-3-q31.explanation:
higher achievements

Đoạn:
“tracking has minimal effects on learning outcomes”

→ very little evidence streaming improves achievement.

→ higher achievements fits summary best.

→ Đáp án: higher achievements

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-3-q32.explanation:
bottom sets

Đoạn:
“negatively affect those students assigned to the lowest sets”

→ lowest sets = bottom sets.

→ Đáp án: bottom sets.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-3-q33.explanation:
disadvantaged backgrounds

Đoạn:
“higher representation of low socioeconomic class”

→ low socioeconomic class = disadvantaged backgrounds.

→ Đáp án: disadvantaged backgrounds.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-3-q34.explanation:
brightest pupils

Đoạn:
“small benefit for those lucky clever students”

→ clever students = brightest pupils.

→ Đáp án: brightest pupils.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-3-q35.explanation:
lower expectations

Đoạn:
“Streaming ... limits what the teacher feels the student is capable of.”

→ teachers expect less from streamed students.

→ Đáp án: lower expectations.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-3-q36.explanation:
Writer says:
“If we accept this model, it follows that streaming students ... would be efficient”

→ Vygotsky model actually supports streaming, not mixed-ability classes.

→ NO.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-3-q37.explanation:
Bài nói:
“value of knowledgeable student peers must not go unrecognised”

→ writer supports peer MKO roles.

Không có thông tin teachers uncertain about it.

→ NOT GIVEN.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-3-q38.explanation:
Đoạn:
“There is also something exciting about passing on skills and knowledge that you yourself have just mastered”

→ teaching recently learned knowledge is rewarding/exciting.

→ YES.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-3-q39.explanation:
Writer concludes:
“we need the many to flourish – not suffer at the expense of a few bright stars.”

→ writer does NOT think priority should only be top students.

→ NO.

catalog-cam-18-3-reading-part-3-q40.explanation:
Writer mentions class hikes as analogy/example of cooperation.

But no direct evidence that outdoor activities improve classroom academic outcomes.

→ NOT GIVEN.
